meaning: adj.折衷的,折衷学派的n.折衷主义者,折衷派的人
examples: She has very eclectic tastes in literature.
In addition, he seems to be pursuing his own eclectic vision without giving a moment's thought to such established competitors as Connosisseur and Horizon.
The contemporary drag show features top Indigenous drag queens, musicians and award-winning filmmakers in an eclectic, theatrical performance.
' This is more like the artistic studio where you have an eclectic mixture.
